issues.title: Test file name does not match source file name
issues.convention: Test files must have the same name as the source file they test (e.g. MyComponent.test.tsx for MyComponent.tsx)
issues.fix: Either rename this test file to CartDrawer.test.tsx (merging with the existing CartDrawer.test.tsx), or extract a CartDrawerWithItems.tsx component and keep the test as-is. Currently there is no CartDrawerWithItems.tsx source file but both CartDrawer.test.tsx and CartDrawerWithItems.test.tsx exist — both import from ./CartDrawer.

patterns.description: All component (.tsx) files outside Next.js app router directories consistently use PascalCase. App router files (page.tsx, layout.tsx, error.tsx, providers.tsx, global-error.tsx) correctly use lowercase Next.js convention.
All hook files in */hooks/* directories consistently start with 'use' prefix followed by PascalCase (e.g. useAuditLog.ts, useProductMutations.ts). No violations found.
All utility files in */utils/* directories consistently use camelCase (e.g. computeRole.ts, formatLastSeen.ts, getApiBasePath.ts). No violations found.
All test files for hooks and components follow the <SourceName>.test.<ext> pattern. One exception found (NAME-001).
